tiers.tier_1: MVP — artist+label feeds, novelty feeder, detail panel, persistence, all accessibility/error/empty handling
tiers.tier_1_5: Shop watch — ships when ≥8 candidate shops validate to a working auto-feed source
tiers.tier_2: Should-have next iteration (expanded adjacency, notes/rating, library-suggests-shops, weekly digest, label-rename watchdog, background pagination, compilation-master_id enrichment)
tiers.tier_3: Stretch / future (friends lane, Bandcamp daily, generic HTML scrape, Beatport/Juno, auto-download)

critical_path_note: UI tasks T-024 through T-037 all touch docs/index.html and depend on prior DOM/state foundations; they cannot truly parallelize for a single dev. Backend chain ~15h + UI chain ~36h ≈ 50h sequential.

honest_effort_estimate_weekends.tier_1_full_time: 6-9 weekends (16-24 productive hours each) with 1.5x unknown-unknowns buffer
honest_effort_estimate_weekends.tier_1_part_time_evenings: 12-18 weeks (8-12 hours per week) — realistic 3-4 months
honest_effort_estimate_weekends.tier_1_5: +1-2 weekends conditional on validation gate passing

notes: Each task carries a realistic hours estimate. Tasks were pressure-tested through 7 grill iterations; see .agent/prd/iteration-log.md for design archaeology.
Critical path is documented in critical_path_chain — these tasks must execute in order. Other tasks parallelize.
Implementation order: start at T-001 and follow critical_path_chain; parallel-track tasks (T-005, T-006, T-011, T-012, T-013) can begin once their direct dependencies complete.

tasks.title: Create autocue/analysis/discover/ package skeleton
tasks.description: Create package directory with empty __init__.py exporting public surface (TasteVector, build_taste_vector, score_release, assemble_feed, DiscoverStore). Add subdirs: feeders/, feeders/shops/, migrations/, shops/.
pass_criteria: python -c 'from autocue.analysis.discover import TasteVector, build_taste_vector, DiscoverStore' succeeds (after later tasks define these)

tasks.title: Style adjacency: JSON resource + strict validation + bundled default (v0.4 — S3-8 + S3-9)
tasks.description: style_graph.py: STYLE_ALIAS_MAP (normalize 'Deep House' / 'deep-house' / 'deephouse' → 'deep_house') + load_style_adjacency() function that: (1) attempts to load autocue/analysis/discover/style_adjacency.json (user-editable, W-NEW-4); (2) validates against style_adjacency.schema.json with strict JSON Schema (S3-8); (3) on any failure (missing file, malformed JSON, schema violation), falls back to bundled-default style_adjacency.default.json and sets a server-level flag readable via /api/discover/stats so UI can surface 'using bundled defaults' warning; (4) provides STYLE_ADJACENCY.get(style, []) semantics on lookup (S3-9 — unknown style returns empty list, no KeyError). The user JSON has each entry with 'edges' list + 'terminal' bool. Anchor styles (≥3 edges) and terminal styles (0-2 edges) per S-NEW-1. Tier 1 ships ~60 styles in the default JSON (~30 anchor + ~30 terminal).
pass_criteria: STYLE_ALIAS_MAP has ≥60 keys
style_adjacency.default.json validates against schema
Every anchor style has ≥3 edges (validated by test)
Drum & Bass, Footwork, Jungle, Garage UK, IDM are all anchor styles
S3-8 fallback test: malformed user JSON → load returns default + warning flag set; server does not crash
S3-9 unknown-style test: STYLE_ADJACENCY.get('nonexistent_style', []) returns []; no KeyError

tasks.title: Implement TasteVector + build_taste_vector
tasks.description: TasteVector dataclass (artists Counter, labels Counter, styles Counter, bpm_hist list, key_hist Counter). build_taste_vector(db, include_streaming=False): plays-weighted artist Counter (log(1+plays)), labels (log(1+total_plays)*sqrt(track_count) with track-count fallback), normalized styles via STYLE_ALIAS_MAP, BPM histogram (buckets of 4, BPM=0 excluded), Camelot key histogram. Streaming-source tracks filtered unless include_streaming=True. AutoCue-namespaced My Tags only (use ALL_AUTOCUE_TAG_NAMES allowlist).
pass_criteria: TasteVector built from mocked Rekordbox DB has populated artists/labels/styles/bpm_hist/key_hist
Streaming-source tracks excluded when include_streaming=False (verified by test)
User-created My Tags are NOT in styles Counter (only AutoCue tags)

tasks.title: Unit tests for taste vector + style graph
tasks.description: tests/test_discover_taste.py covers plays-weighted scoring math, log-damping, cold-start fallback to track count, streaming filter, style normalization, BPM=0 exclusion. tests/test_discover_style_graph.py covers normalize_style() idempotence, alias-map round-trip, adjacency-graph sanity (no self-loops, symmetric where intended).
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_discover_taste.py tests/test_discover_style_graph.py -v all green

tasks.title: Add Discogs client functions for v2 (v0.8 — master_id retained for Tier 2 enrichment, no longer required at scan-time dedup)
tasks.description: Extend autocue/analysis/discogs.py with: search_label_releases(label_id, token, year_from), search_seller_inventory(seller, token, since_date), get_release_details(release_id, token), search_labels(query, token), get_artist_relations(artist_id, token), validate_token(token). All respect existing token bucket. Wrapper reads x-discogs-ratelimit-remaining from every response; raises RateLimitNearExhausted when <5 remaining; raises Discogs429 with retry-after on 429. get_release_details() exposes master_id from the Discogs response — used by the detail panel and (Tier 2) background pagination for empty-artist enrichment.
pass_criteria: Each new function has a happy-path test with mocked HTTP
Rate-limit awareness: client sleeps and continues when remaining<5; aborts cleanly on 429
validate_token returns True for 200 on /oauth/identity, False for 401
S6-2 acceptance: get_release_details mock returns master_id when present in Discogs response; returns None when absent (older entries)

tasks.title: Discogs client tests (extend test_discogs.py)
tasks.description: Mock responses for each new function. Specific rate-limit behavior tests: header parsing, near-exhaustion sleep, 429 abort. Token validation tests for 200 / 401 / network error paths.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_discogs.py passes including all new test cases

tasks.title: Implement artist_feeder + label_feeder (v0.6 — staging-column writes + explicit follow-priority, S3-3 + S5-5)
tasks.description: feeders/artist.py: top-20 artists from taste_vector, 1 request each, page-1 only. feeders/label.py: explicit follow-priority (S3-3) — first fill slots from followed_labels ordered by last_scanned_at ASC NULLS FIRST (W4-1); if budget remains, fill from taste_vector implicit top-N, excluding any already in the explicit list. Total label budget 15 requests. TTL gating reads COMMITTED last_scanned_at (not pending). v0.6 S5-5: feeders call store.mark_label_scanned(label_id, scan_id, scanned_at) and store.mark_artist_scanned(artist_id, scan_id, scanned_at) — these write to last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id columns, NEVER directly to last_scanned_at. The commit-to-last_scanned_at happens atomically at scan finish in scan_orchestrator (T-014). Errors in one feeder don't abort scan — yield ('error', exception_info) sentinel and continue.
pass_criteria: Mocked Discogs responses produce expected releases
TTL respected: feeder skips artist with committed last_scanned_at <24h ago
One feeder failure does not abort the scan (verified by test)
S3-3 explicit follow-priority: with 20 explicit follows + budget=15, scan picks 15 longest-unscanned explicit; over 2 scans, all 20 are eventually scanned (round-robin verified by test)

S5-5 staging-column write: after scan invocation, last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id are SET on scanned entities; last_scanned_at remains unchanged until orchestrator commits (verified by test inspecting both columns mid-scan)

tasks.title: Implement novelty_feeder (v0.4 — sparse-adjacency + unknown-style aware)
tasks.description: feeders/novelty.py. Runs ONE strategy per scan, rotated round-robin via discover.db.scans.novelty_strategy. Budget 10 req/scan. Strategies: (a) style-adjacent — for top-3 taste_vector.styles, use STYLE_ADJACENCY.get(style, []) (S3-9) and fetch from each adjacent style; (b) label-adjacent — top-5 followed labels' parent/sub-labels; (c) artist-adjacent — top-5 taste-vector artists' members/groups. Tags output as source='novelty:style'|'novelty:label'|'novelty:artist'. Sparse pool conditions (return empty + set novelty_status='sparse_adjacency'): (i) top-3 styles all terminal per JSON, OR (ii) top-3 styles all absent from JSON (unknown). Logs unknown_styles list on scan row for UI hint per S3-9.
pass_criteria: Single strategy invoked per scan (verify across 3 sequential scans = 3 different strategies)
Budget ≤10 requests per scan invocation
Sparse case top-3 all terminal: returns empty + sets novelty_status correctly
S3-9 sparse case top-3 all unknown: returns empty + sets novelty_status + scan row has unknown_styles list populated

tasks.title: Implement ranker — conditional Stage 2 reservation (v0.3 — S-NEW-1 fix)
tasks.description: ranker.py. score_release(release, taste_vector) implements the 7-term formula from PRD §6.3 stage 1, applying hard_block from blocked_artists/blocked_labels. assemble_feed(scored_releases, novelty_fraction=0.25, top_n=50) implements stage 2 per PRD §6.3 conditional logic: full reservation when novelty pool ≥ quota, partial when pool is thin (no backfill of novelty slots from retrieval — surfaces what's available + sets scan_status['novelty_partial']), empty pool → pure retrieval with scan_status['novelty_status']='sparse_adjacency' (no garbage backfill).
pass_criteria: score_release returns float in [0,100] always (Hypothesis property)
Full novelty pool: feed has exactly 25% novelty
Thin pool (5 novelty for 12 slots): feed has 5 novelty + 45 retrieval; novelty_partial=5 in status
Empty pool: feed has 50 retrieval, no garbage; novelty_status='sparse_adjacency'
Blocked artist/label never appears in feed

tasks.title: Ranker tests with Hypothesis properties
tasks.description: tests/test_discover_ranker.py — properties: score ∈ [0,100], dismissed lowers score, novelty share guarantee, blocked never appears, snoozed-past-until_date reappears with resurfaced=True flag in card metadata. Plus concrete unit tests for each scoring term.
pass_criteria: Hypothesis suite passes with default ≥100 examples per property

tasks.title: Define discover.db schema + migrations runner + scan-lock crash recovery (v0.5 — staging columns, NULLABLE artist/title, unknown_styles, per-table merge)
tasks.description: store.py with DiscoverStore class. discover_data_dir() helper resolves to platform-native location. No legacy ~/.autocue migration. migrations/001_initial.sql per PRD §6.7 v0.5 schema: saved/dismissed/snoozed/downloaded have release_key as PK + release_key_version INTEGER + NULLABLE artist/title (S4-3 — store coerces empty/null to 'Unknown Artist'/'Unknown Title' at insert); scans has status + unknown_styles columns (C4-2); followed_labels and followed_shops have last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id staging columns (S4-2). Migration runner detects missing schema_version table → applies 001 unconditionally (W-NEW-2). DiscoverStore __init__ runs the v0.5 boot recovery: (1) for crashed scans, clear last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id (per-scan_id, not timestamp-window — S4-2 prevents over-rollback), (2) UPDATE scans SET finished_at=now, status='crashed' WHERE finished_at IS NULL. In one transaction. Save/dismiss/snooze/download CRUD methods coerce empty artist/title to 'Unknown' strings before insert (S4-3).
pass_criteria: First instantiation creates DB at canonical path; schema_version=1
All v0.5 columns present (release_key_version + NULLABLE artist/title on state tables; status + unknown_styles on scans; last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id on followed_labels/shops)
Migration bootstrap: blank DB applies 001 cleanly
Idempotent: running migrations twice doesn't fail

v0.5 S4-2 acceptance: two crashed scans bracket one successful scan. Boot recovery clears pending values of the two crashed scans WITHOUT touching the successful scan's committed last_scanned_at on the same entities
v0.5 S4-3 acceptance: save() with artist='' or artist=None results in stored row with artist='Unknown Artist'; no insert failure

tasks.title: DiscoverStore CRUD + state methods (v0.3: release_key-keyed)
tasks.description: Methods: save(release_key, …)/unsave(release_key)/is_saved(release_key)/list_saved (all keyed by release_key — S-NEW-5), dismiss/undismiss/is_dismissed/list_dismissed (release_key), snooze/list_snoozed (with auto-clear of past until_date on read), record_download/list_downloaded, block_artist/unblock_artist/list_blocked_artists (Discogs artist ID), block_label/unblock_label/list_blocked_labels (Discogs label ID), follow_label/unfollow_label/list_followed_labels (with last_scanned_at update), follow_shop/unfollow_shop/list_followed_shops, start_scan/finish_scan/get_running_scan, record_release_detail/get_release_detail (TTL 30d), record_youtube_results(release_key, track_index, …)/get_youtube_results, increment_shop_health/reset_shop_health. Also: saves_correlated_to_scan(scan_id) implementing the timestamp-window query (S-NEW-8) — returns saves where saved_at BETWEEN scan.started_at AND scan.finished_at + 30min.
pass_criteria: Each method covered by unit test; round-trip CRUD verified
is_saved(release_key) returns True for a sibling-pressing release_id once the canonical key is saved (the S-NEW-5 acceptance test)
saves_correlated_to_scan computes the timestamp window correctly across boundary cases

tasks.title: DiscoverStore tests
tasks.description: tests/test_discover_store.py — in-memory SQLite. Cover every method, snooze auto-clear, release-detail TTL, scan-in-progress detection, migration replay from blank.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_discover_store.py passes

tasks.title: Concurrent-scan lock + dedup pipeline + staging-column commit (v0.7 — master_id-aware compilation dedup, S6-2)
tasks.description: scan_orchestrator.py coordinates feeders. Acquires store.start_scan() lock (writes scan_id, finished_at NULL). Streams feeder yields through dedup using release_key normalized from RAW artist/title + master_id (S5-2 + v0.7 S6-2). Feeders pass through master_id from Discogs (search_label_releases / search_seller_inventory already return it). For empty-artist releases, release_key uses '\u200B__compilation__\u200B|||{title}|||mid_{master_id}' when master_id is present; falls back to '...|||rid_{release_id}' when master_id is null — preserves format-variant dedup for compilations with master_ids (1981 vs 2024 reissue of same comp → one card) while still blocking cross-compilation collisions. Excludes library_album_set() + dismissed/snoozed/downloaded/blocked. On clean finish: store.commit_pending_scan(scan_id) atomically promotes last_scanned_at_pending → last_scanned_at on every followed_label/followed_shop where pending_scan_id=this_scan_id (S5-5). Then store.finish_scan(scan_id, status='ok', stats). On cancel/error: store.finish_scan(scan_id, status='cancelled'|'rate_limited', ...); pending cols stay for boot recovery to clear. Releases lock in finally.
pass_criteria: Concurrent invocation blocks/returns 409 (verified by test using threading)
Dedup key normalization folds 'Sam Gendel' / 'sam gendel ' / 'SAM GENDEL' to same key

v0.7 S6-2 acceptance: two compilation releases with same title + same master_id (1981 vinyl + 2024 reissue) dedup to ONE card (format-variant invariant preserved); two compilation releases with same title + DIFFERENT master_ids stay as TWO cards (cross-comp collision still blocked); compilation with null master_id falls back to release_id discriminator
Scan stats persist regardless of success/failure
S5-5 acceptance: on scan finish ok, pending values atomically promote to last_scanned_at in one transaction (verified by SQL inspection)

tasks.title: GET /api/discover/feed (SSE) — main feed endpoint
tasks.description: Streams scan progress + ranked releases as SSE. Query params: sources[], format, year, style[], sort, explore. Returns 409 immediately if a scan is already running (concurrent-scan lock). Events: {progress:{feeder,scanned,total}}, {release:{...full release card payload}}, {done:{scan_id,duration_ms,stats}}.
pass_criteria: FastAPI TestClient consumes SSE: receives ≥1 progress + ≥1 release + 1 done
Second concurrent request returns 409 with scan_id payload

tasks.title: Discover state endpoints (CRUD)
tasks.description: POST /api/discover/save, /dismiss, /snooze (with duration param), /unsave, /undismiss, /unsnooze, /block-artist, /unblock-artist, /block-label, /unblock-label. GET /api/discover/saved, /dismissed, /snoozed, /downloaded, /blocked-artists, /blocked-labels.
pass_criteria: Each endpoint covered by TestClient
Snooze duration accepts '1w'|'1m'|'3m' → maps to days correctly

tasks.title: Discover follow-labels endpoints
tasks.description: GET /api/discover/labels/suggested (top-20 unwatched library labels by plays-weighted score). POST /api/discover/labels/follow (label_id + name). POST /api/discover/labels/unfollow. Reuses existing search_labels for autocomplete via GET /api/discover/labels/search?q=.
pass_criteria: TestClient covers all four endpoints

tasks.title: Release detail + scan-control endpoints
tasks.description: GET /api/discover/releases/{id} (cached via release_details table; on miss, fetches from Discogs). GET /api/discover/feed/status (current scan + per-feeder progress). POST /api/discover/feed/cancel (sets finished_at on running scan_id).
pass_criteria: Detail cache hit returns instantly; miss triggers Discogs fetch
Cancel during scan completes within 2s and returns 200

tasks.title: State export / import endpoints
tasks.description: POST /api/discover/state/export → returns gzip-tar of discover.db (Content-Disposition: attachment). POST /api/discover/state/import → accepts uploaded tar.gz, validates SQLite header magic on extraction, atomically swaps in (close existing connection, replace file, reopen). Refuses files >50MB.
pass_criteria: Export returns valid gzip
Import rejects non-SQLite file with 400
Round-trip: export → import → existing rows preserved

tasks.title: Discover stats endpoint (telemetry surface)
tasks.description: GET /api/discover/stats. Returns: total scans, avg duration, saves-per-scan-90d, novelty-share-90d, top 10 saved labels, top 10 saved artists, current followed counts. All read-only over discover.db.
pass_criteria: TestClient returns expected shape on a seeded DB

tasks.title: Route tests for /api/discover/*
tasks.description: Extend tests/test_serve_routes.py with TestClient cases for every new endpoint above. Specifically cover concurrent-scan 409, SSE event shapes, snooze duration parsing, blocked-artist enforcement in feed, import-rejects-non-sqlite.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_serve_routes.py passes with all new cases

tasks.title: Backup integration: parallel sidecar with two-phase atomicity + SQLCipher-aware validator (v0.7 — S6-1)
tasks.description: Extend backup machinery to write discover.db as parallel sidecar at BACKUP_DIR/discover_<timestamp>.db (matching master_<timestamp>.db). v0.4 two-phase atomicity (S3-6): CREATE writes both as .tmp files, fsync, then atomic rename; on second-rename failure, immediately delete the first. DELETE renames both to .deleting then unlinks; startup cleanup pass finishes orphan .deleting/.tmp removals. RESTORE: copy both to temp paths, verify with per-db validator, atomically rename in place. **v0.7 S6-1 per-db validator**: discover.db verified via SQLite magic header check (bytes[:16] == b'SQLite format 3\x00'). master.db verified by passing the EXPLICIT SQLCipher key from app.state.db._key (path-agnostic — does not rely on pyrekordbox's path-based auto-detect) into either Rekordbox6Database(presnap_path, key=app.state.db._key) or a direct sqlcipher3.connect() + PRAGMA key + SELECT 1 FROM djmdContent LIMIT 1. Required because BACKUP_DIR is not under the Rekordbox install root, so path-fingerprint auto-detection would fail there. On second rename failure, restore original master from a pre-snapshot temp file. Pre-v2 backups (master only) restore cleanly. /api/backups list returns unified records {timestamp, master_path, discover_path|null}.
pass_criteria: Backup writes two parallel files with matching timestamp
Pre-v2 backup (master only) restores cleanly
DELETE removes both atomically

S3-6 atomicity tests: simulated rename failure on second file leaves no half-state; startup cleanup removes orphan .tmp/.deleting files; restore failure on second file rolls back master to original via pre-snapshot temp

S6-1 acceptance: validator opens an encrypted snapshot from BACKUP_DIR (path NOT under Rekordbox install root) using explicit key kwarg from app.state.db._key; succeeds when key is correct, raises clearly when key is wrong

tasks.title: Token validation: 1h positive cache + instant-401-invalidate (v0.3 — S-NEW-6 fix)
tasks.description: Extend /api/status (or add /api/discover/token-status) with Discogs token validation result. Cache structure: {valid: True, checked_at} for up to 1h. ANY 401 from ANY Discogs API call (across feeders, autocomplete, release-details, etc.) immediately invalidates the cache and triggers /api/status to return {valid: false} on next check. Frontend banner appears within one round-trip of token revocation, not 1h later.
pass_criteria: Endpoint returns {valid:true} on 200 from /oauth/identity
Endpoint returns {valid:false} immediately after a 401 from any Discogs call
Cache prevents repeated /oauth/identity calls within 1h on positive path

tasks.title: Rewrite Discover tab structure (no more ↓ Album button)
tasks.description: docs/index.html: TAB_CONTENTS.discover replaced. Layout: filter bar / settings sub-panel toggle / feed grid / detail panel slide-out. Card structure: artwork, title, artist, label chip, source badge(s), hover-reveal action row (💚 save / 💤 snooze / ✕ dismiss / more menu). NO download button on the card.
pass_criteria: Manual: no download button visible on cards
Hover-reveal actions present
Source badges render correctly for multi-source releases

tasks.title: Detail panel with tracklist + dialog semantics
tasks.description: _openReleaseDetailPanel(release_id). Slide-out right panel (full-screen on <900px). role='dialog', aria-modal='true', focus trap on open, focus return to triggering card on close, Esc key closes. Fetches /api/discover/releases/{id} for tracklist; renders loading state during fetch.
pass_criteria: Manual: panel opens via card click and Enter key; Esc closes; focus returns to card
Mobile: full-screen panel on viewport <900px (use Chrome DevTools device emulation)

tasks.title: Inline YouTube preview carousel — LAZY policy (v0.3 — S-NEW-4 fix)
tasks.description: _renderYouTubeCarousel(release_key, track_index). On panel open, pre-warm YouTube search for TRACK #1 ONLY. All other tracks render with a 'Find' hint state; their ▶ button fires the search on user click — prevents per-panel-open yt-dlp storm (S-NEW-4 fix). Calls /api/youtube/search → 3-result carousel per track. Each iframe is www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/{videoId}?autoplay=0&modestbranding=1 with allow='autoplay; encrypted-media' and sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation'. 'Try another' button cycles to next candidate. iframe error handler auto-advances; on exhaustion shows '[Search YouTube] [Search Bandcamp]' fallback links. Results disk-cached in discover.db.youtube_results keyed by (release_key, track_index) — survives reload.
pass_criteria: Manual: panel open triggers ONE YouTube search (track 1), not 12
Click ▶ on track 5 triggers search at click time
Test: open panel, count /api/youtube/search calls = 1 (track 1 only)
Reload preserves cached results

tasks.title: Detail panel Download buttons + Shift-click power flow (v0.3 — W-NEW-1 fix)
tasks.description: 'Download all' button → confirm modal → fetches /api/download/album. 'Download selected' with per-track checkboxes → /api/download per track. Disabled with tooltip if /api/download/config reports yt-dlp+ffmpeg missing. Card-level Shift+click handler bypasses panel: opens confirm modal with **default focus on CANCEL button** (W-NEW-1 fix — prevents sticky-Shift + accidental-Enter from triggering download). User must Tab to or click Download to proceed.
pass_criteria: Manual: Download buttons fire only after explicit click
Shift+click confirm modal has Cancel as default focus (Tab order verified)
Pressing Enter immediately on modal open triggers Cancel, not Download (verified by test)
Disabled state visible when yt-dlp absent

tasks.title: Keyboard shortcuts
tasks.description: _setupKeyboardShortcuts(): j/k navigate feed (selected card outline), Enter open panel, s save, x dismiss, z snooze (opens duration popover), D Download all (panel-open required), Esc close panel. Shortcut help: '?' key shows a small modal listing all shortcuts.
pass_criteria: Manual: each shortcut works as documented; '?' modal renders

tasks.title: Filter bar + sort + Explore mode
tasks.description: Sticky bar above feed: Source chips (Artist/Label/Shop/Novelty multi-select), Format select, Year select, Style multi-select (populated from current results), Sort dropdown (Taste match / Newest / Title / Artist / Explore mode). Each change calls /api/discover/feed with new query params. Persists per-session in sessionStorage, not localStorage.
pass_criteria: Each filter triggers refresh and the new URL params hit the backend
Explore mode flips novelty fraction to 0.5 via backend param

tasks.title: Settings sub-panel: Followed Labels
tasks.description: _renderLabelSettings(). Sections: 'Watched labels' (followed list with remove), 'Suggested from your library' (top-20 unwatched, one-click add), 'Search & add' (debounced autocomplete via /api/discover/labels/search). Each row shows last_scanned_at + health badge.
pass_criteria: Manual: type label name → autocomplete → click → label appears in watched list; persists across reload

tasks.title: Onboarding banner for first-run (zero followed labels)
tasks.description: If GET /api/discover/labels lists nothing, show a top-of-feed banner: 'Pick labels from your library to watch' + top-10 chips with Add buttons + 'Add all'. Banner self-dismisses after first follow.

tasks.description: _renderBlockedList(). Two columns (artists / labels) with unblock buttons. Empty state copy: 'Nothing blocked. You can 🚫 block from a card\'s More menu.'
pass_criteria: Manual: block from card → appears in this list → unblock → disappears

tasks.title: Snooze popover + resurface badge
tasks.description: Clicking 💤 on a card opens a small popover with 1w / 1m / 3m buttons (default highlighted: 1m). Selection POSTs to /api/discover/snooze. When a card carries resurfaced=True (set by backend on feed events for releases past their until_date), render a '🔁 You snoozed this on {date}' chip; remove the chip once the card has been interacted with (or after 60s in view).
pass_criteria: Manual: snooze 1m → release disappears; force date forward (or seed DB) → release reappears with badge

tasks.title: Scan-progress UI + Refresh button + delta count
tasks.description: Feed top shows a small status row: 'Refreshed 2h ago — 4 new since last refresh' with manual [Refresh] button. During scan, replaced by per-feeder progress bar reading from the SSE stream. Concurrent-scan-running state shows 'Scan in progress (started 12s ago)' + [Cancel].
pass_criteria: Manual: trigger scan, see progress; cancel mid-scan; refresh after; delta count shown

tasks.title: Empty states + error states
tasks.description: Empty: no followed labels (onboarding banner), no results after filters (clear-filters CTA), all releases snoozed/dismissed ('You're caught up — enable Explore mode or follow more labels'). Error: Discogs token invalid (banner with re-enter link), Discogs rate-limit hit mid-scan (banner: 'Discogs is rate-limiting us — try again in 1 minute'), one feeder failed (small 'Shop watch had errors — see details' link → modal with feeder errors).
pass_criteria: Manual: each empty/error state reachable and renders the right copy/CTA

tasks.title: Export / Import state UI
tasks.description: In Discover settings: 'Sync between machines' card with [Export discover.db] button (calls /api/discover/state/export → downloads gz) and [Import] file input (calls /api/discover/state/import). On import success: confirm modal showing pre/post counts, then full app reload.
pass_criteria: Manual: round-trip export + import preserves saved/dismissed counts

tasks.title: Discover stats surface
tasks.description: Small 'Stats' link in Discover settings → modal/section showing scan count, avg scan duration, saves-per-scan, novelty share, top saved labels/artists. Read from /api/discover/stats.
pass_criteria: Manual: stats render after seeding the DB with sample scans+saves

tasks.title: Vitest tests for Discover frontend
tasks.description: tests/web/discover.test.js — copy and test: _renderDiscoverCard (source badges, action chips), _renderYouTubeCarousel (3-candidate cycling, error fallback), Shift+click handler, keyboard-shortcut handler, focus trap on detail panel, _renderResurfaceBadge. tests/web/discover_filters.test.js — filter matrix + sort + Explore mode params.
pass_criteria: npm test passes

tasks.title: Playwright e2e smoke for Discover
tasks.description: tests/e2e/discover.spec.ts: open Discover → progress bar appears → at least 1 release renders → save one → reload → still saved → dismiss → no resurface in next scan → snooze → simulate +30d via env var → resurface badge appears. RUN_FULL=1 gated (writes).
pass_criteria: RUN_FULL=1 playwright test discover.spec.ts passes

tasks.title: Hard 60-request scan cap + per-feeder budget + rate-limit backoff (v0.3 — C-NEW-1 fix)
tasks.description: Implement the 60-request hard cap from PRD §8 v0.3 Constraints in scan_orchestrator: artists=20, labels=15, shops=15, novelty=10. Each Discogs call increments scan.requests_used; orchestrator refuses to dispatch any feeder request beyond budget — silently skips with metric. Integrate Discogs client's RateLimitNearExhausted (when x-discogs-ratelimit-remaining<5, sleep 5s; on 429, abort scan with status='rate_limited'). Verify scan never exceeds the hard cap in a stress test with synthetic large library (100 artists / 50 labels) — only top-20 / top-15 reached.
pass_criteria: scan.requests_used ≤ 60 at end-of-scan (Hypothesis property)
Stress test: 100-artist library completes with only top-20 artists scanned, requests_used=60
Simulated 429 mid-scan: scan finishes status='rate_limited' with clear error surfaced

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md with Discover v2 architecture
tasks.description: Add the autocue/analysis/discover/ tree, the new /api/discover/* endpoints, the platform-native data dir, the feedparser dep, the discover.db schema sketch, and all new test files. Run bash .claude/hooks/check_doc_sync.sh → 0 gaps.
pass_criteria: bash .claude/hooks/check_doc_sync.sh reports zero gaps

tasks.title: Update docs/FEATURES.md + new docs/reference/discover-v2.md
tasks.description: FEATURES.md: user-facing copy for the new flow (follow labels, preview, save/snooze/block, Shift-click, keyboard shortcuts, Export/Import). discover-v2.md: long-form covering shop source-type model, novelty graph, validate_pack.py usage, troubleshooting (Discogs token invalid, rate-limited, scan stuck).
pass_criteria: Markdown preview readable; covers all flows in §7

tasks.title: Author candidate_pack.json (30 shops)
tasks.description: autocue/analysis/discover/shops/candidate_pack.json — the 30-row candidate list from PRD §6.10 with display_name, city, country, homepage_url_guess, preferred_strategy hint. Strategy hint guides the validate script's probe order.
pass_criteria: JSON parses; ≥30 rows; each row has display_name + at least one of {discogs_handle_guess, homepage_url_guess, bandcamp_handle_guess}

tasks.title: Implement shop source-type probe helpers
tasks.description: feeders/shops/{discogs_seller,rss,bandcamp,manual}.py — each implements a Source protocol: probe(spec) → {ok, items_count_or_none} and fetch(spec, since) → list[release]. feeders/rss_autodiscover.py: <link rel=alternate>, common paths /feed /rss /atom.xml /blogs/news.atom. All HTTP with 10s timeout, 5MB body cap, polite UA.
pass_criteria: Each source-type probe + fetch covered by mocked-HTTP test
rss_autodiscover finds feeds in 3 different realistic page samples

tasks.title: Implement validate_pack.py + CLI command
tasks.description: shops/validate_pack.py — given candidate_pack.json, probe each row: Discogs seller (num_for_sale ≥ 5), then RSS auto-discover from homepage_url_guess, then Bandcamp, else manual. Write starter_pack.json with successful matches + source_type. Expose as `autocue discover validate-shops` CLI subcommand.
pass_criteria: CLI invocation produces starter_pack.json
Output report counts {discogs, rss, bandcamp, manual}
Polite: ≥1.5s gap between requests

tasks.title: Shop-source + validate-pack tests
tasks.description: tests/test_discover_shop_sources.py, tests/test_discover_rss_autodiscover.py, tests/test_discover_validate_pack.py. Mock HTTP for each source path. Validate the polite-pause behavior, error tolerance, and starter_pack.json shape.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_discover_shop_sources.py tests/test_discover_rss_autodiscover.py tests/test_discover_validate_pack.py passes

tasks.title: Implement shop_feeder (multi-source) — staging-column writes (v0.6 S5-5)
tasks.description: feeders/shop.py: for each followed_shop in store, dispatch on source_type → call appropriate Source.fetch(). TTL gating reads COMMITTED last_scanned_at (6h). On scan completion of a shop, call store.mark_shop_scanned(shop_id, scan_id, scanned_at) which writes to last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id (NEVER directly to last_scanned_at — S5-5). Update health column directly on result (errors bump consecutive_errors; 3rd consecutive error → health='error'). Yield (release, source='shop:{display_name}:{source_type}').
pass_criteria: Mocked sources produce expected releases; failing source eventually marked health=error after 3 attempts
S5-5 acceptance: shop scan writes to last_scanned_at_pending + pending_scan_id, not directly to last_scanned_at

tasks.title: Shop endpoints
tasks.description: GET /api/discover/shops/candidates (returns candidate_pack.json merged with current validation status from starter_pack.json). POST /api/discover/shops/validate (SSE: runs validate_pack.py, streams per-shop probe results). POST /api/discover/shops/probe (URL → detected source_type, no persist). POST /api/discover/shops/follow / /unfollow. GET /api/discover/shops/followed.
pass_criteria: TestClient covers all endpoints incl. validate SSE
Probe endpoint doesn't persist anything

tasks.title: Settings sub-panel: Shops
tasks.description: _renderShopSettings(). 'Watched shops' (followed list with source-type icon, last_scanned_at, health badge, remove). 'Candidate shops' (list from /candidates, toggle to follow). 'Add by URL' (paste URL → /probe → confirm detected source → follow). 'Validate starter pack' button → runs SSE validation with progress.
pass_criteria: Manual: validate-pack runs and updates UI; toggling a candidate makes it watched; pasting a URL detects RSS
